scope_note: Database / SQL: SELECT, filtering, aggregation, joins, DDL, DML.

knowledge_units.name: Basic SELECT
knowledge_units.example_correct_code: SELECT * FROM t

knowledge_units.name: Select Specific Columns
knowledge_units.example_correct_code: SELECT a, b FROM t

knowledge_units.name: FROM Clause
knowledge_units.example_correct_code: SELECT * FROM users

knowledge_units.name: WHERE Clause
knowledge_units.example_correct_code: SELECT * FROM t WHERE x > 0

knowledge_units.name: Comparison in WHERE
knowledge_units.example_correct_code: SELECT * FROM t WHERE a = 1

knowledge_units.name: ORDER BY
knowledge_units.example_correct_code: SELECT * FROM t ORDER BY a

knowledge_units.name: ORDER BY ASC/DESC
knowledge_units.description: ASC and DESC in ORDER BY.
knowledge_units.example_correct_code: SELECT * FROM t ORDER BY a DESC

knowledge_units.name: LIMIT
knowledge_units.example_correct_code: SELECT * FROM t LIMIT 10

knowledge_units.name: COUNT()
knowledge_units.example_correct_code: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM t

knowledge_units.name: SUM and AVG
knowledge_units.example_correct_code: SELECT SUM(x), AVG(x) FROM t

knowledge_units.name: GROUP BY
knowledge_units.example_correct_code: SELECT a, COUNT(*) FROM t GROUP BY a

knowledge_units.name: HAVING
knowledge_units.example_correct_code: SELECT a, COUNT(*) FROM t GROUP BY a HAVING COUNT(*) > 1

knowledge_units.name: INNER JOIN
knowledge_units.description: Join tables with INNER JOIN ON.
knowledge_units.example_correct_code: SELECT * FROM a INNER JOIN b ON a.id = b.a_id

knowledge_units.name: LEFT JOIN
knowledge_units.example_correct_code: SELECT * FROM a LEFT JOIN b ON a.id = b.a_id

knowledge_units.name: Subquery in WHERE
knowledge_units.example_correct_code: SELECT * FROM t WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM u)

knowledge_units.name: DISTINCT
knowledge_units.example_correct_code: SELECT DISTINCT a FROM t

knowledge_units.name: CREATE TABLE
knowledge_units.example_correct_code: CREATE TABLE t (id INT, name TEXT)

knowledge_units.name: PRIMARY KEY
knowledge_units.example_correct_code: CREATE TABLE t (id INT PRIMARY KEY)

knowledge_units.name: FOREIGN KEY
knowledge_units.example_correct_code: CREATE TABLE t (a_id INT REFERENCES p(id))

knowledge_units.name: SQL Data Types
knowledge_units.example_correct_code: CREATE TABLE t (x INT, y TEXT)

knowledge_units.name: INSERT
knowledge_units.example_correct_code: INSERT INTO t (a, b) VALUES (1, 'x')

knowledge_units.name: UPDATE
knowledge_units.description: Update rows with UPDATE SET WHERE.
knowledge_units.example_correct_code: UPDATE t SET a = 1 WHERE b = 2

knowledge_units.name: DELETE
knowledge_units.description: Delete rows with DELETE FROM WHERE.
knowledge_units.example_correct_code: DELETE FROM t WHERE a = 0

knowledge_units.name: NULL and IS NULL
knowledge_units.description: Check for NULL with IS NULL.
knowledge_units.example_correct_code: SELECT * FROM t WHERE x IS NULL

knowledge_units.name: AND / OR in WHERE
knowledge_units.example_correct_code: SELECT * FROM t WHERE a = 1 AND b > 0

knowledge_units.name: IN Clause
knowledge_units.example_correct_code: SELECT * FROM t WHERE id IN (1, 2, 3)

knowledge_units.name: LIKE Pattern
knowledge_units.description: LIKE for pattern matching with % and _.
knowledge_units.example_correct_code: SELECT * FROM t WHERE name LIKE 'A%'

knowledge_units.name: BETWEEN
knowledge_units.example_correct_code: SELECT * FROM t WHERE x BETWEEN 1 AND 10

knowledge_units.name: Subquery in SELECT
knowledge_units.example_correct_code: SELECT a, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM b WHERE b.a_id = a.id) FROM a

knowledge_units.name: CREATE VIEW
knowledge_units.example_correct_code: CREATE VIEW v AS SELECT * FROM t

knowledge_units.name: CREATE INDEX
knowledge_units.example_correct_code: CREATE INDEX idx_t_a ON t(a)

knowledge_units.name: BEGIN/COMMIT
knowledge_units.example_correct_code: BEGIN; INSERT INTO t VALUES (1); COMMIT;
